title: adora-rs dataflow specification
description:
Defines an Adora dataflow: nodes, their connections, and execution parameters.
See https://adora-rs.ai/concepts/dataflow-yaml/ for the full guide.

nodes.description: List of nodes in the dataflow. Each node must have a unique `id`.

health_check_interval.description: How often (seconds) the daemon sweeps node health. For each node with `health_check_timeout`, the daemon checks whether it has communicated within its timeout; if not, the node is killed and its restart_policy evaluated. Default: 5.0.

_unstable_debug.description: Debug options for development and troubleshooting (unstable).

Node.title: Adora Node
Node.description: A computational unit in a dataflow. Runs as a separate process (or hosts operators in a shared runtime).

id.description: Unique node identifier. Must not contain `/` characters.

name.description: Human-readable display name (metadata only, used in tooling and logs).

description.description: Documentation string (metadata only, not used at runtime).

path.description: Path to the executable or script. Can be a local path or URL. On Windows, `.exe` is appended automatically if the file has no extension.

args.description: Command-line arguments passed to the executable (space-separated).

git.description: Git repository URL. `adora build` clones the repo and uses it as the working directory for `build` and `path`.

branch.description: Git branch to checkout. Requires `git`. Mutually exclusive with `tag` and `rev`.

tag.description: Git tag to checkout. Requires `git`. Mutually exclusive with `branch` and `rev`.

rev.description: Git revision (commit hash) to checkout. Requires `git`. Mutually exclusive with `branch` and `tag`.

build.description: Build commands run during `adora build`. Each line runs as a separate command. `pip`/`pip3` lines are replaced by `uv pip` when `--uv` is passed.

inputs.description: Input subscriptions. Keys are local input IDs, values are `<node-id>/<output-id>` strings or objects with options.

outputs.description: Output data identifiers produced by this node.

output_types.description: Optional type annotations for outputs. Maps output IDs to type URNs (e.g. `std/media/v1/Image`).

input_types.description: Optional type annotations for inputs. Maps input IDs to expected type URNs for validation.

env.description: Environment variables for build and execution. Values can be strings, numbers, or booleans. Use `__adora_env` to read from host environment.

send_stdout_as.description: Route raw stdout/stderr lines as a data output with this ID. Each line is sent as a separate Arrow message.

send_logs_as.description: Route structured log entries as a data output with this ID. Each entry is a JSON string with fields: timestamp, level, node_id, message, target, fields.

min_log_level.description: Suppress logs below this level from file output, coordinator forwarding, and send_logs_as routing. From most to least verbose: stdout (all output including raw stdout), trace, debug, info, warn, error.

max_log_size.description: Maximum log file size before rotation (e.g. "50MB", "1GB").

max_rotated_files.description: Maximum number of rotated log files to keep. Default: 5.

restart_policy.description: When to restart the node on exit.

max_restarts.description: Maximum restart attempts. 0 = unlimited. Combine with `restart_window` for "N restarts per M seconds".

restart_delay.description: Initial delay (seconds) before restarting. Doubles each attempt (exponential backoff).

restart_window.description: Time window (seconds) for counting restarts. Counter resets after this many seconds since the first restart in the current window. Combine with max_restarts for 'N restarts per M seconds'.

health_check_timeout.description: If the node does not communicate with the daemon (send outputs, subscribe, etc.) for this many seconds, the daemon kills the process and evaluates the restart_policy.

operator.description: Single operator configuration (shorthand for `operators` with one entry).

operators.description: Multiple operators sharing a runtime process.

ros2.description: ROS2 bridge configuration (unstable). Declares this node as an automatic ROS2 bridge.

custom.description: Legacy node configuration. Deprecated -- use top-level fields instead.

Input.description: An input subscription. Either a short-form string (`node-id/output-id`, `adora/timer/millis/N`, or `adora/logs[/level[/node]]`) or an object with `source`, `queue_size`, `queue_policy`, and `input_timeout`.

source.description: Source in the same format as the short form string.

queue_policy.description: Queue overflow policy. 'drop_oldest' (default) drops the oldest message when full. 'backpressure' buffers up to 10x queue_size without dropping (drops with ERROR log at hard cap).

input_timeout.description: Circuit breaker timeout in seconds. If no message arrives within this period, the daemon closes the input and the node receives an InputClosed event for graceful degradation.

EnvValue.description: Environment variable value. Supports strings, integers, floats, and booleans. Environment variable expansion with `$VAR` syntax is supported.

anyOf.description: Read value from host environment variable.

__adora_env.description: Name of the host environment variable to read.

RestartPolicy.description: When a node should be restarted.

OperatorDefinition.description: An operator running inside a shared runtime process.

id.description: Unique operator identifier within the runtime node.

inputs.description: Input subscriptions (same format as node inputs).

shared-library.description: Path to a shared library (.so/.dylib/.dll) implementing the operator.

python.description: Python operator source. Either a path string or an object with `source` and optional `conda_env`.

send_stdout_as.description: Route stdout as a data output.

send_logs_as.description: Route structured log entries as a data output.

min_log_level.description: Minimum log level for this operator.

max_log_size.description: Maximum log file size before rotation.

max_rotated_files.description: Maximum rotated log files to keep.

SingleOperatorDefinition.description: Single operator configuration (convenience for runtime nodes with one operator).

shared-library.description: Path to a shared library implementing the operator.

send_stdout_as.description: Route stdout as a data output.

send_logs_as.description: Route structured log entries as a data output.

max_log_size.description: Maximum log file size before rotation.

max_rotated_files.description: Maximum rotated log files to keep.

PythonSource.description: Python operator source. Either a path string or an object with `source` and optional `conda_env`.

Ros2BridgeConfig.description: ROS2 bridge configuration. Exactly one of `topic`, `topics`, `service`, or `action` must be set.

topic.description: Single ROS2 topic name (e.g. "/camera/image_raw"). Mutually exclusive with `topics`, `service`, `action`.

message_type.description: ROS2 message type (e.g. "sensor_msgs/Image"). Required when `topic` is set.

direction.description: subscribe (ROS2 -> Adora) or publish (Adora -> ROS2). Default: subscribe.

topics.description: Multiple topics on a single ROS2 node context. Mutually exclusive with `topic`, `service`, `action`.

service.description: ROS2 service name (e.g. "/add_two_ints"). Mutually exclusive with `topic`, `topics`, `action`.

service_type.description: ROS2 service type (e.g. "example_interfaces/AddTwoInts"). Required when `service` is set.

action.description: ROS2 action name (e.g. "/navigate"). Mutually exclusive with `topic`, `topics`, `service`.

action_type.description: ROS2 action type (e.g. "nav2_msgs/NavigateToPose"). Required when `action` is set.

role.description: Client or server. Required for `service` and `action`.

qos.description: QoS policies applied to all topics (can be overridden per-topic).

node_name.description: ROS2 node name. Defaults to the adora node id.

Ros2TopicConfig.description: Configuration for a single ROS2 topic in multi-topic mode.

reliable.description: Use reliable transport. Default: false (best effort).

lease_duration.description: Lease duration in seconds. Default: infinity.

max_blocking_time.description: Max blocking time in seconds for reliable transport.

keep_last.description: History depth for KeepLast policy. Default: 1.

keep_all.description: Use KeepAll history policy instead of KeepLast.

Deploy.description: Deployment configuration for distributing nodes across machines.

machine.description: Target machine/daemon ID. The coordinator routes the node to the daemon registered with this ID.

labels.description: Labels for label-based scheduling (e.g. `gpu: "true"`, `arch: arm64`). Matched against daemon labels.

distribute.description: How built binaries reach remote daemons. local: each daemon builds from source; scp: CLI pushes binary via SSH/SCP; http: daemon pulls from coordinator artifact store.

publish_all_messages_to_zenoh.description: Publish all messages to Zenoh for debugging (required for `adora topic echo/hz/info`).
